messages.content: You are the Host Forensics Expert. Target OS: Windows. Vector Space: 4D windows_math. Source: Sysmon event stream. Schema: sysmon_event_id, Image, CommandLine, ParentImage, User, IntegrityLevel, TargetImage, GrantedAccess, TargetObject, Details, ImageLoaded, Signed, PipeName, QueryName, TargetFilename. Identify persistence tradecraft. Output MITRE ATT&CK + containment recommendation.

Spatial Anomaly Detected.
Source: sysmon_sensor
Vector: <|spatial_vector|>
Windows Host — Registry Run Key Persistence.
Host: APP-84  User: schen
  EventID: 4657
  RegistryKey: HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run
  ValueName: OneDriveSync
  ValueData: C:\Users\Public\helper.bat
  WrittenBy: EXCEL.EXE  (unsigned_binary=True)
  boot_persistence=YES  (survives reboot)

<analysis>
[AXIS 1] Benign Alternative Assessment:
  Legitimate software that adds Run key entries does so from a signed installer (msiexec, setup.exe) pointing to C:\Program Files\<Vendor>\. Parent process EXCEL.EXE is not an installer — it is a script host or Office process.
[AXIS 2] Behavioral Proof Assessment:
  Parent=EXCEL.EXE (adversarial loader, not software installer). ValueData='C:\Users\Public\helper.bat' (%TEMP%/%APPDATA% path = not a vendor installation directory). unsigned_binary=True. Key=HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run (user/system-wide autorun). This combination — script-host parent + temp path + unsigned binary — has no legitimate software analog.
[AXIS 3] Entity Coverage:
  Host APP-84 will execute the binary at C:\Users\Public\helper.bat on every login/boot. Persistence is established. Follow-on C2 activity expected.
[CONCLUSION] Registry Run key persistence confirmed.
</analysis>
TRUE POSITIVE. MITRE T1547.001 (Boot/Logon Autostart: Registry Run Keys). Remove registry value, delete payload binary, isolate host.
RECOMMENDED_ACTION: contain

Spatial Anomaly Detected.
Source: sysmon_sensor
Vector: <|spatial_vector|>
Windows Host — Registry Run Key Write.
  RegistryKey: HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run
  ValueName: Slack
  ValueData: C:\Program Files\Slack\slack.exe
  WrittenBy: msiexec.exe  signed=True
  change_ticket=CHG-12957  deployment_source=SCCM

<analysis>
[AXIS 1] Benign Alternative Assessment:
  msiexec writing to HKLM Run for a signed application in C:\Program Files — standard software installation.
[AXIS 2] Behavioral Proof Assessment:
  parent=msiexec.exe (software installer). ValueData=C:\Program Files\ (vendor installation path). signed=True. Ticket CHG-12957. SCCM-deployed.
[AXIS 3] Entity Coverage:
  Authorized software deployment — no adversarial characteristics.
[CONCLUSION] Authorized software installer adding startup entry — signed binary, standard path, change ticket.
</analysis>
FALSE POSITIVE. T1547.001 — AUTHORIZED SOFTWARE INSTALL. No action.
RECOMMENDED_ACTION: dismiss
